trans-unit.source: Import a Word document (DOCX), Adobe InDesign markup (IDML), HTML, XLIFF, PO, Excel (XLSX), or PowerPoint (PPTX). Extracted via the Okapi sidecar; round-trips back to the original format on export.
Import a GNU gettext message catalogue (Linux/Django/WordPress software localisation)
Re-import an edited Supervertaler bilingual Word table to update this project's translations and comments.
Re-import an edited [SEGMENT NNNN] text file (with its .svexport.json sidecar) to update the current project's translations and comments.
Open the Supported File Formats reference in your browser
Export the translation back to a document. Output format is the same as the original source (DOCX → DOCX, IDML → IDML, etc.). Uses the Okapi sidecar to preserve formatting.
Export segments as a one-way bilingual Markdown table for AI translation/review (not re-importable)
Export the project back to a GNU gettext .po catalogue, preserving comments, references and flags
Export multi-file project to folder with separate files
Repoint to moved/renamed source folder for multi-file project
Export a re-importable Word bilingual table (inline tags shown as literal text). Edit the Target/Comments columns, then re-import to update this project.
Export a re-importable [SEGMENT NNNN] text file with a .svexport.json sidecar. Edit the target lines (or have an LLM do it), then re-import to update this project.
Translate segments with empty target (useful after partial batch translation)
Translate only segments currently visible after filtering
Copy source text to target for selected/filtered segments
Copy source to target for segments containing no letters (numbers, codes, punctuation only) and mark as Translated

Send/update confirmed (and other selected-status) segments into a writable Translation Memory. With overwrite on, same-source entries are replaced so the TM keeps only the latest translation. (Formerly 'Send Segments to TM'.)
Remove formatting tags from selected segments
Use AI to proofread and verify translation quality
Dock the panel above the translation grid
Dock the panel below the translation grid
Automatically resize all rows to fit content
Private notes for this project (never exported to CAT tools)
Open the session log in a separate window that can be moved to another screen
Open help for the focused panel (F1)
Open the Workbench documentation in your browser
Run the initial setup wizard (data folder location, features overview)
Check whether you are running the latest Supervertaler release
Copy version and system info to clipboard (useful for support)
Open the log file containing startup output and errors – useful when reporting issues
Reveal the folder that contains the diagnostic log files
No translatable strings found in the .po file.

Successfully imported {n} segment(s) from gettext .po.
Languages: {src} → {tgt}
Pretranslated: {n} segment(s) with target text loaded.
Fuzzy: {n} segment(s) marked draft (clear the fuzzy flag by editing).

To export to gettext .po format, please select the original .po (or .pot) file.
This is the file you originally imported.
Would you like to select it now?

Successfully exported {n} translated segment(s) to gettext .po.
File: {name}
